lessons: Dmei havra'a 451.5 pending Ministry of Labor extension order; flip next cycle if signed.
Deferred MAJOR: add illegal-deduction / benefit-bundling / misclassification screens.
Deferred MAJOR: maternity-dismissal protection check; payslip-right check.

cycles.summary: Expert review failed on 3 CRITICAL coverage gaps; added detectable red-flag rows + brief detection rules for all five deferred items.
resolved: CRITICAL: illegal wage deductions / penalty clauses now flagged as a blocker (Wage Protection Law 1958 s.25; lawful deductions are a closed list, employee-authorized debt capped at 25%).

CRITICAL: maternity/pregnancy dismissal protection now flagged; dismissal/pay-cut during pregnancy, maternity leave, or fertility treatment at 6+ months tenure requires a Ministry of Labor heter (Employment of Women Law 5714-1954); absence of a carve-out is flagged as a protection gap.
CRITICAL: right to a detailed monthly payslip now flagged; cash-pay / payslip-silent contract is a red flag (Wage Protection Law Amendment 24, 2008).
MAJOR: all-inclusive salary / benefit-bundling clause flagged as void to cogent components (pension, havra'a, overtime, vacation funded separately).
MAJOR: misclassification (employee vs kablan) now uses the subordination/integration test (mivchan ha'hishtalvut) with retroactive-reclassification risk, not a bare attorney punt.
deferred_minors: MINOR: encode the full sick-pay ladder (day one unpaid, days two-three at half pay, day four onward at full pay) as a presence/rate check, not just an 18-per-year accrual sanity check.
MINOR: tighten the total-employer-cost ratio into an explicit band (roughly 120 to 125 percent of gross) with a callout when figures fall outside it.
MINOR: add an MCP-reference verification note so israel-law / kolzchut citations are spot-checked against the live source before being quoted as authoritative.
